title: Patch::Keep serde error fires at runtime, not compile time
description: A contributor who adds a new Patch<T> field to a patch struct without #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Patch::is_keep")] gets no compile-time feedback. The error surfaces only when that field is serialized at runtime with a Keep value. In practice this means the bug only appears when the user does NOT change a field — the happy path (setting every field) passes all tests. The error message text is actionable but the failure mode is delayed and hard to reproduce in tests.
